item.title: Rips appear in Reflecting Pool's new sealant after algae turn water green
item.description: President Trump's efforts to spruce up the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool faced a snag this week, with algae turning the water green while rips appeared in an "American Flag Blue" surface picked by the president.

item.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
item.description: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

item.title: Kennedy Center still weighing "partial closure," Trump admin. tells judge
item.description: The Trump administration told a federal judge that the Kennedy Center is still weighing whether to offer a full slate of performances or more limited programming over the coming months.

item.title: Peeled paint appears in Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool after algae bloom
item.description: President Trump's multi-million dollar makeover of the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool is not going swimmingly. CBS News' Mark Strassmann reports.

item.title: Some turn to fitness dating apps to meet someone, New York Times reports
item.description: The New York Times reports some singles are turning to fitness dating apps to find a relationship. Danielle Friedman, a contributor for the Times who wrote the piece, joins "The Daily Report" to discuss.

item.title: 1 killed in large fire at luxury resort, several others injured
item.description: One person was killed and several others were hurt when a fire broke out at a luxury beach resort in the Dominican Republic on Friday, local officials said.

item.title: How Trump's Iran goals have changed
item.description: With a memorandum of understanding in place between the U.S. and Iran, "The Daily Report" takes a look at where things stand on some of the goals the Trump administration laid out for the conflict. CBS News' Sam Vinograd also joins with analysis.

item.title: Massive fire at luxury resort in Dominican Republic, 1 dead
item.description: One person was killed and several others were hurt in a massive fire at a luxury beach resort in the Dominican Republic on Friday, local officials said.

item.title: 1 killed, dozens injured after trains collide in England, British officials say
item.description: At least one person was killed and dozens more were injured after two trains collided north of London on Friday, British officials said.

item.title: Trains collide in England, at least 1 killed, 89 injured
item.description: Warning: Graphic video. Two passenger trains collided Friday in Bedford, England, outside of London. At least one person was killed and 89 others were injured, officials said.

item.title: "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" guests for June 21, 2026
item.description: This week's guests include Republican Sen. Lindsey Graham and Democratic Rep. Jason Crow.

item.title: Given 8 months to live, a teen beat cancer thanks to an extraordinary friendship
item.description: In 2022, at the age of 14, Dylan Mwaniki was diagnosed with Stage 4 kidney cancer. Four years later, he graduated from high school.

item.title: James Burrows, co-creator of "Cheers" and prolific director, dies at 85
item.description: James Burrows directed more than 1,000 episodes of television, including every episode of the original "Will & Grace."

item.title: "Cheers" co-creator James Burrows dies at 85
item.description: Director James Burrows co-created the landmark comedy "Cheers" and directed more than 50 pilot episodes. He died at 85 on Friday.

item.title: Company owned by Trump donor won $1.7 million no-bid Reflecting Pool contract
item.description: The federal government awarded a company owned by a Trump donor $1.7 million to install a new water cleaning system for the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool, records show.

item.title: U.S. clinches spot in World Cup knockout round with dominant win over Australia
item.description: In front of a roaring Seattle crowd, the U.S. men's soccer team​ on Friday defeated Australia in its second World Cup match, clinching a spot in the Round of 32 in the process.

item.title: Doctor goes above and beyond for her cancer patient, ending with a big surprise
item.description: Steve Hartman goes "On the Road" with a story of a teen battling for his life and the doctor who made him a promise.

item.title: Trump unveils new Air Force One, a $400 million plane gifted by Qatar
item.description: "This is considered the world's most luxurious plane," the president said in front of the enormous new jet.

item.title: New problems arise for the Reflecting Pool after Trump's renovations
item.description: All week, Americans have heard about the stubbornly green Reflecting Pool on the National Mall. Now, the Trump administration said the water is a "crystal clear" blue. Mark Strassmann double-checks.

item.title: Trump unveils new Air Force One, gifted from Qatar
item.description: President Trump unveiled what's meant to become the new Air Force One, a gift from the government of Qatar. Plus, new fighting erupted between Israel and Lebanon before they signed a new ceasefire. Nicole Killion has all this and more from the White House.

item.title: Details on Israel and Hezbollah's ceasefire amid delayed U.S.-Iran talks
item.description: Israel and Hezbollah have agreed to a ceasefire in Lebanon as officials say fighting between them has delayed U.S.-Iran peace talks. CBS News' Imtiaz Tyab and Margaret Brennan report.

item.title: 6/19: CBS Evening News
item.description: The U.S. Men's soccer team advances in the World Cup; a deadly train collision occurred in London.

item.title: U.S. defeats Australia in World Cup, clinching spot in knockout round
item.description: For the first time since 1930, the U.S. Men have won their first two matches of a World Cup. They not only beat Australia, but also clinched a spot in the knockout round. Nicole Valdes has more.

item.title: Trump unveils Qatari-gifted jet serving as new Air Force One
item.description: President Trump on Friday unveiled the new Air Force One, a $400 million Boeing 747-8 jet, gifted by Qatar.

item.title: Expert warns of "bumpy road ahead" for Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire as U.S. seeks Iran deal
item.description: CBS News contributor Aaron MacLean is reacting to the latest Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire news after tensions rose in Lebanon, likely leading to U.S.-Iran talks being postponed.

item.title: How the Iran war united, and then divided Trump and Netanyahu
item.description: Trump has appeared during the Iran war to lose patience with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, who may now find himself "stuck."

item.title: What's missing from the Epstein files? Here's what we found
item.description: The Justice Department says it's released "every document required by the Epstein Files Transparency Act," but CBS News has identified numerous gaps.

item.title: Scientists discover parasite that preys on insect-killing "zombie fungus"
item.description: Scientists say the "hyperparasite" preys on "zombie fungus" known to infect insects before subjecting them to a gruesome death.

item.title: Less than half of Americans can afford quality healthcare, Gallup finds
item.description: Gallup found that only 49% of Americans were "cost-secure" last year, with concerns about medical bills and prescription costs rising across income groups.

item.title: Homeland security chief reacts after Somalian referee was denied U.S. entry for World Cup
item.description: Homeland Security Secretary Markwayne Mullin is reacting to backlash after the U.S. denied entry for Somalian referee Omar Arta to participate in the FIFA World Cup. CBS News' Camilo Montoya-Galvez reports.
